---
title: "conjunction_pvalue"
generated: true
description: "Conjunction p-value across `k` contrasts."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:conjunction_pvalue -->
```python
conjunction_pvalue(p_values: Float[Array, 'k *spatial']) -> Float[Array, '*spatial']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:conjunction_pvalue -->
Conjunction p-value across `k` contrasts.

Returns the per-element maximum of the per-contrast p-values, the p-scale
dual of [`conjunction`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons/conjunction.qmd). The minimum-statistic conjunction is significant
at level $\alpha$ if and only if every contrast's p-value is below
$\alpha$, equivalently if and only if their maximum is, so the maximum
p-value is the valid conjunction p-value.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:conjunction_pvalue -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `k` × `*spatial` | — | The `k` per-contrast p-value maps stacked on the leading axis, each of shape `*spatial`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `*spatial`. The per-element maximum p-value over the `k` contrasts.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:conjunction_pvalue -->
### References

Nichols, T., Brett, M., Andersson, J., Wager, T., & Poline, J.-B. (2005).
Valid conjunction inference with the minimum statistic. NeuroImage, 25(3),
653-660. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2004.12.005
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:conjunction_pvalue -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
